goal: architecture-todo-impl: Implement all items in docs/todo/architecture-todo.md until the architecture backlog is complete (close or ship each actionable theme row; update docs/status per repo AGENTS.md when backlog entries move). Run the orchestrate loop until finished.
summary: Close every actionable architecture backlog theme with code, tests, and status-doc hygiene.
acceptanceCriteria: All actionable rows in docs/todo/architecture-todo.md are either shipped or explicitly closed with rationale and supporting evidence
GA blocker release-todo §1 mutation lifecycle is fully functional end-to-end (proposal emission, accept/reject resume, apply, and undo semantics)
Theme 3/4/6 residual architecture work is implemented or decisively closed with truthful status/docs updates
Backlog hygiene in AGENTS.md is satisfied: status docs updated and docs/todo/product-done.md receives one-line closure notes for newly closed architecture entries
Relevant FE and BE tests for changed surfaces pass with evidence captured in handoffs

tasks.name: close-theme5-mutation-lifecycle
tasks.scopedGoal: Implement Theme 5 / release-todo §1 end-to-end: emit mutation proposals, wire FE apply path and resume accept/reject, add undo semantics, and ship tests proving idempotent replay does not re-apply.
tasks.brief: Implement the remaining GA blocker in code across backend and frontend contracts. Required outcomes: (1) a typed backend mutation-proposal payload/event that is emitted on relevant chat-agent turns, (2) FE-interrupt contract that can apply approved mutations through a concrete `fe.applyMutation` tool path (or equivalent registered FE tool), (3) accept/reject resume flow using LangGraph Command(resume=...) with persisted proposal identifiers, (4) server-side undo semantics that the FE toast can invoke safely, (5) audit/analytics for accepted mutations and undos, and (6) targeted tests for proposal->accept->apply plus idempotent replay safety. Keep solution production-safe under existing idempotency + auth + autonomy constraints.
acceptance: A remote chat/session can emit a mutation proposal, user acceptance resumes the graph, and the approved mutation is actually applied
Reject path exits cleanly without side effects; undo path can reverse an accepted mutation within the documented semantics
Replay/idempotency tests prove accepted mutations are not double-applied on retries/resume
tasks.verify: Run the most direct FE+BE suites for touched files (unit/integration as needed) and include concrete command outputs showing proposal emission, accept/reject resume behavior, apply path, and undo/replay correctness.

tasks.name: verify-theme5-mutation-lifecycle
tasks.scopedGoal: Independently verify that Theme 5 / release-todo §1 is truly closed end-to-end and not just partially wired.
tasks.brief: Audit the target branch for backend event contracts, FE handling, resume/apply/undo semantics, and tests. Fail verification if any path is stubbed, gated-only, or non-functional. Specifically confirm idempotency safety (no double mutation on replay), clear reject behavior, and a callable undo lifecycle.
acceptance: Verifier confirms full proposal->accept->apply->undo lifecycle works with runtime evidence
Verifier confirms replay/idempotency behavior prevents double apply

tasks.name: close-remaining-architecture-themes
tasks.scopedGoal: Implement or decisively close all actionable residual work in architecture themes 1, 2, 3, 4, and 6 beyond Theme 5, with code/tests/runbooks as needed.
tasks.brief: Treat docs/todo/architecture-todo.md as the source backlog and close each still-actionable row outside Theme 5. Prioritize: contract/error hardening residuals (Theme 1/2), FE surface simplification residuals (Theme 3: stable deps sweep, adapter thinning, useAi/useAgent divergence reduction, capability gating), durable resume/state continuity + operator decision-tree docs (Theme 4), and Theme 6 depth rows where practical. If a Theme 6 row should be closed as deferred/non-actionable now, do it explicitly with concrete rationale and no misleading claims. Ship code + tests for implementation rows; avoid touching docs/todo files in this task.
acceptance: Residual Theme 1/2/3/4/6 actionable items have concrete implementation evidence or explicit closure rationale ready for status-doc integration
No remaining unstable `useAgent` consumer effect dependency loops or autonomy-capability drift in active surfaces
Operator-facing docs for resume/idempotency/failure policy are updated when behavior changes
tasks.verify: Run targeted FE/BE checks for every changed subsystem and include command outputs plus a row-by-row closure mapping from architecture themes to concrete code/doc artifacts.

tasks.name: verify-remaining-architecture-themes
tasks.scopedGoal: Independently verify closure quality for non-Theme5 architecture rows.
tasks.brief: Check whether each residual actionable row in themes 1/2/3/4/6 is actually closed by implementation evidence or explicit justified closure. Fail with exact row-level gaps if any claim lacks supporting code/tests/docs.
acceptance: Verifier returns pass only if every residual actionable architecture row has defensible closure status

tasks.name: integrate-architecture-backlog-closeout
tasks.scopedGoal: Integrate completed architecture branches into one coherent branch, reconcile conflicts, and update architecture/release/product status docs so the architecture backlog is complete and truthful.
tasks.brief: Start from the strongest implementation branch and integrate the other verified branch's commits. Resolve conflicts without regressing behavior. Then perform AGENTS.md backlog hygiene in docs/todo: update architecture-todo rows to closed/shipped status, reflect any moved/deferred rows with rationale, keep release-todo/product-done aligned, and add one-line product-done entries for newly closed backlog items. Ensure final docs are truthful on the integrated baseline and reference verification logs where needed instead of brittle hard-coded counts.
acceptance: Integrated branch contains all verified architecture implementations with no unresolved conflicts
docs/todo/architecture-todo.md has no remaining actionable open rows
docs/todo/product-done.md includes one-line entries for all newly closed architecture backlog work and remains consistent with release/status docs
tasks.verify: Run targeted FE/BE checks impacted by integration and include row-by-row mapping showing each architecture action row and its closure evidence on the integrated branch.

tasks.name: verify-architecture-backlog-complete
tasks.scopedGoal: Final independent verification that architecture backlog closure is complete, accurate, and regression-safe.
tasks.brief: Audit integrated code and docs. Confirm docs/todo/architecture-todo.md has no remaining actionable open rows, release/product docs are aligned, AGENTS backlog hygiene is met, and key tests covering changed surfaces pass. Fail with specific blockers if any residual architecture action remains unresolved or mis-documented.
acceptance: Verifier confirms architecture backlog is complete and status docs are aligned
Verifier confirms no unresolved actionable architecture rows remain
